# SponJIT stencil normal-form foundation
#
# Maintained build artifacts are SSA-consumed stencil normal forms emitted by
# src/worker_compile.lua into per-worker SQLite DBs, globally deduped before
# descriptor bank C generation.

SPONGEJIT := $(dir $(abspath $(lastword $(MAKEFILE_LIST))))
SPONGEJIT := $(SPONGEJIT:%/=%)
ROOT := $(SPONGEJIT)/../..
LUA := luajit
LUAROCKS_ENV := eval "$$(luarocks --lua-version=5.1 path 2>/dev/null || luarocks path)" &&

.PHONY: all test stencils bank clean

all: test

stencils:
	cd $(SPONGEJIT) && ./build_stencils.sh

bank:
	cd $(SPONGEJIT) && ./build_bank.sh

test:
	cd $(ROOT)/.. && $(LUAROCKS_ENV) $(LUA) experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/tests/test_spongejit_real_ssa.lua
	cd $(ROOT)/.. && $(LUAROCKS_ENV) $(LUA) experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/tests/test_spongejit_stencil_desc.lua
	cd $(ROOT)/.. && $(LUAROCKS_ENV) $(LUA) experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/tests/test_spongejit_stencil_projection.lua
	cd $(ROOT)/.. && $(LUAROCKS_ENV) $(LUA) experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/tests/test_spongejit_stencil_lower.lua
	cd $(ROOT)/.. && $(LUAROCKS_ENV) $(LUA) experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/tests/test_spongejit_retirement.lua

clean:
	rm -rf $(SPONGEJIT)/build

bench/
build/
build_bank.sh
build_stencils.sh
foundry.lua
include/
Makefile
puc/
runtime/
src/

else
        nforms = nforms + 1
        forms_seen[dedupe_key] = true
        local form = {
          key = normal_key,
          dedupe_key = dedupe_key,
          stencil_hash = r.stencil_hash,
          stencil_form = r.stencil_form,
          stencil_key = r.stencil_key,
          stencil_ops = r.stencil_ops,
          stencil_slotmaps = r.slotmaps,
          ops = ops,
          facts = facts,
          contract = contract,
          count = 1,
          changed = r.changed,
          source_ops = ops,
        }
        local ssa_payload = {
          stencil = r.stencil,
          stencil_hash = r.stencil_hash,
          stencil_key = r.stencil_key,
          stencil_ops = r.stencil_ops,
          slotmaps = r.slotmaps,
          active_node_specs = r.active_node_specs,
          checked_facts = r.checked_facts,
          deps = r.deps,
          projection = r.projection,
          source_ops = ops,
          facts = facts,
        }
        local selector_sig = tostring(contract.selector_sig and contract.selector_sig.literal or "0x0000000000000000ULL")
        insert_form:bind_values(dedupe_key, normal_key, ckey, selector_sig, 1, cjson.encode(form), cjson.encode(ssa_payload))
        step_done(insert_form)
      end
    end
  end
  if progress_every > 0 and (si % progress_every == 0 or si == #seqs) then
    io.stderr:write(string.format("[W%d] %d/%d compiles=%d ssa_ok=%d unique_forms=%d\n", ci, si, #seqs, lc, lssa_ok, nforms))
  end
end

insert_form:finalize()
bump_form:finalize()
insert_pattern:finalize()
local stats = assert(db:prepare([[INSERT INTO stats(key, value) VALUES (?, ?)]]))
local ssa_rejected = lc - lssa_ok
for _, kv in ipairs({
  {"seqs", #seqs},
  {"compiles", lc},
  {"ok", lssa_ok},
  {"ssa_ok", lssa_ok},
  {"rejected", ssa_rejected},
  {"forms", nforms},
  {"stencils", 0},
}) do
  stats:bind_values(kv[1], kv[2])
  step_done(stats)
end
stats:finalize()
assert(db:exec("COMMIT; CREATE INDEX forms_stencil_hash_idx ON forms(stencil_hash); CREATE INDEX patterns_ops_key_idx ON patterns(ops_key);") == sqlite3.OK)
db:close()

io.stderr:write(string.format("[W%d] DONE seqs=%d compiles=%d ssa_ok=%d rejected=%d unique_forms=%d db=%s\n", ci, #seqs, lc, lssa_ok, ssa_rejected, nforms, db_path))

-- dedupe_normal_forms.lua -- Global foundry normal-form merge + unique lowering.
--
-- This is the semantic boundary that the monolithic foundry implies:
--   opcode sequence + facts -> SSA consumes semantics -> canonical normal form
--   -> global equivalence class -> lower one representative to the descriptor bank.
--
-- Per-worker DBs contain normal forms and pattern aliases. This script merges
-- equivalent forms across workers, lowers each unique final form once, and keeps
-- all source-pattern aliases so the C selector can map many opcode sequences to
-- the same unique bank id.

package.path = 'src/?.lua;src/?/init.lua;' .. package.path

local cjson = require("cjson")
local sqlite3 = require("lsqlite3")
local Util = require("src.util")
local StencilLower = require("src.stencil_lower")
local StencilDesc = require("src.stencil_desc")

local tmpdir = arg[1] or os.getenv("SPON_TMP") or "build/stencil_bank"
local chunks = tonumber(arg[2] or os.getenv("CHUNKS") or "128") or 128
local out_path = arg[3] or (tmpdir .. "/stencil_bank.sqlite")

local function worker_path(ci) return tmpdir .. "/grammar_worker_" .. ci .. ".sqlite" end

local function step_done_for(db, stmt)
  local rc = stmt:step()
  if rc ~= sqlite3.DONE then error(db:errmsg()) end
  stmt:reset()
end

local function numeric_key_map(t)
  if type(t) ~= "table" then return t end
  local out = {}
  for k, v in pairs(t) do out[tonumber(k) or k] = v end
  return out
end

local function rehydrate_ssa_payload(p)
  p = p or {}
  local st = p.stencil or {}
  st.slot_class_by_concrete = numeric_key_map(st.slot_class_by_concrete)
  st.slot_concrete_by_class = st.slot_concrete_by_class or {}
  p.stencil = st
  return p
end

os.remove(out_path)
local db = assert(sqlite3.open(out_path))
assert(db:exec([[
PRAGMA journal_mode = OFF;
PRAGMA synchronous = OFF;
PRAGMA temp_store = MEMORY;
PRAGMA locking_mode = EXCLUSIVE;
CREATE TABLE unique_forms (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  dedupe_key TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  stencil_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
  contract_key TEXT NOT NULL,
  selector_sig TEXT NOT NULL,
  count INTEGER NOT NULL,
  first_chunk INTEGER NOT NULL,
  form_json TEXT NOT NULL,
  ssa_json TEXT NOT NULL,
  lower_ok INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  bank_id INTEGER UNIQUE,
  stencil_json TEXT,
  lower_errors_json TEXT
);
CREATE TABLE form_patterns (
  dedupe_key TEXT NOT NULL,
  ops_key TEXT NOT NULL,
  ops_json TEXT NOT NULL,
  count INTEGER NOT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY(dedupe_key, ops_key)
);
CREATE TABLE chunk_stats (
  chunk INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  seqs INTEGER NOT NULL,
  compiles INTEGER NOT NULL,
  ok INTEGER NOT NULL,
  ssa_ok INTEGER NOT NULL,
  rejected INTEGER NOT NULL,
  forms INTEGER NOT NULL,
  stencils INTEGER NOT NULL
);
CREATE TABLE bank_stats (
  key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  value INTEGER NOT NULL
);
BEGIN IMMEDIATE;
]]) == sqlite3.OK)

local insert_unique = assert(db:prepare([[INSERT INTO unique_forms
  (dedupe_key, stencil_hash, contract_key, selector_sig, count, first_chunk, form_json, ssa_json)
  VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
  ON CONFLICT(dedupe_key) DO UPDATE SET count = count + excluded.count]]))
local insert_pattern = assert(db:prepare([[INSERT INTO form_patterns
  (dedupe_key, ops_key, ops_json, count) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
  ON CONFLICT(dedupe_key, ops_key) DO UPDATE SET count = count + excluded.count]]))
local insert_chunk = assert(db:prepare([[INSERT INTO chunk_stats
  (chunk, seqs, compiles, ok, ssa_ok, rejected, forms, stencils)
  VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)]]))

local totals = {seqs = 0, compiles = 0, ok = 0, ssa_ok = 0, rejected = 0, forms = 0, stencils = 0, patterns = 0}

for ci = 1, chunks do
  local path = worker_path(ci)
  assert(Util.path_exists(path), "missing worker db: " .. path)
  local w = assert(sqlite3.open(path))
  local stats = {}
  for row in w:nrows("SELECT key, value FROM stats") do stats[row.key] = tonumber(row.value) or 0 end
  insert_chunk:bind_values(ci, stats.seqs or 0, stats.compiles or 0, stats.ok or 0, stats.ssa_ok or 0, stats.rejected or 0, stats.forms or 0, stats.stencils or 0)
  step_done_for(db, insert_chunk)
  totals.seqs = totals.seqs + (stats.seqs or 0)
  totals.compiles = totals.compiles + (stats.compiles or 0)
  totals.ok = totals.ok + (stats.ok or 0)
  totals.ssa_ok = totals.ssa_ok + (stats.ssa_ok or 0)
  totals.rejected = totals.rejected + (stats.rejected or 0)
  totals.forms = totals.forms + (stats.forms or 0)
  totals.stencils = totals.stencils + (stats.stencils or 0)

  local n = 0
  for row in w:nrows("SELECT dedupe_key, stencil_hash, contract_key, selector_sig, count, form_json, ssa_json FROM forms ORDER BY rowid") do
    insert_unique:bind_values(row.dedupe_key, row.stencil_hash, row.contract_key, row.selector_sig, tonumber(row.count) or 1, ci, row.form_json, row.ssa_json)
    step_done_for(db, insert_unique)
    n = n + 1
    if n % 50000 == 0 then io.stderr:write(string.format("[dedupe] chunk %d merged %d forms\n", ci, n)) end
  end

  local pn = 0
  for row in w:nrows("SELECT dedupe_key, ops_key, ops_json, count FROM patterns ORDER BY rowid") do
    insert_pattern:bind_values(row.dedupe_key, row.ops_key, row.ops_json, tonumber(row.count) or 1)
    step_done_for(db, insert_pattern)
    pn = pn + 1
  end
  totals.patterns = totals.patterns + pn
  w:close()
  io.stderr:write(string.format("[dedupe] chunk %d/%d forms=%d patterns=%d compiles=%d\n", ci, chunks, stats.forms or 0, pn, stats.compiles or 0))
end

insert_unique:finalize()
insert_pattern:finalize()
insert_chunk:finalize()
assert(db:exec("COMMIT;") == sqlite3.OK)

assert(db:exec([[
CREATE INDEX unique_forms_stencil_hash_idx ON unique_forms(stencil_hash);
CREATE INDEX unique_forms_count_idx ON unique_forms(count);
CREATE INDEX unique_forms_bank_id_idx ON unique_forms(bank_id);
CREATE INDEX form_patterns_ops_key_idx ON form_patterns(ops_key);
CREATE INDEX form_patterns_dedupe_key_idx ON form_patterns(dedupe_key);
BEGIN IMMEDIATE;
]]) == sqlite3.OK)

local update_lower = assert(db:prepare([[UPDATE unique_forms
  SET lower_ok = ?, bank_id = ?, stencil_json = ?, lower_errors_json = ? WHERE id = ?]]))
local unique_forms, lowered_stencils, lower_rejected = 0, 0, 0
for row in db:nrows("SELECT id, form_json, ssa_json FROM unique_forms ORDER BY id") do
  unique_forms = unique_forms + 1
  local id = tonumber(row.id)
  local form = assert(cjson.decode(row.form_json))
  local ssa = rehydrate_ssa_payload(assert(cjson.decode(row.ssa_json)))
  local lower = StencilLower.generate(ssa, { facts = form.facts or ssa.facts or {} })
  if lower.ok then
    local stencil = lower.stencil
    lowered_stencils = lowered_stencils + 1
    stencil.stencil_id = lowered_stencils
    stencil.abi = StencilDesc.lower_to_abi(stencil)
    update_lower:bind_values(1, lowered_stencils, cjson.encode(stencil), nil, id)
  else
    update_lower:bind_values(0, nil, nil, cjson.encode(lower.errors or {}), id)
    lower_rejected = lower_rejected + 1
  end
  step_done_for(db, update_lower)
  if unique_forms % 50000 == 0 then
    io.stderr:write(string.format("[dedupe] lowered %d unique forms, stencils=%d rejected=%d\n", unique_forms, lowered_stencils, lower_rejected))
  end
end
update_lower:finalize()
assert(db:exec("COMMIT;") == sqlite3.OK)

local pattern_aliases = 0
for row in db:nrows("SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM form_patterns") do pattern_aliases = tonumber(row.n) or 0 end

local stat_stmt = assert(db:prepare("INSERT INTO bank_stats(key, value) VALUES (?, ?)"))
local function stat(k, v)
  stat_stmt:bind_values(k, v or 0)
  step_done_for(db, stat_stmt)
end
stat("chunks", chunks)
stat("seqs", totals.seqs)
stat("compiles", totals.compiles)
stat("ok", totals.ok)
stat("ssa_ok", totals.ssa_ok)
stat("ssa_rejected", totals.rejected)
stat("worker_unique_forms", totals.forms)
stat("unique_forms", unique_forms)
stat("lowered_stencils", lowered_stencils)
stat("lower_rejected", lower_rejected)
stat("pattern_aliases", pattern_aliases)
stat_stmt:finalize()
db:close()

io.stderr:write(string.format("[dedupe] DONE chunks=%d compiles=%d worker_forms=%d unique_forms=%d lowered_stencils=%d pattern_aliases=%d db=%s\n",
  chunks, totals.compiles, totals.forms, unique_forms, lowered_stencils, pattern_aliases, out_path))

count = counts.stencils,
    endpoints = counts.endpoints,
    locations = counts.locations,
    data_relocs = counts.data_relocs,
    control_relocs = counts.control_relocs,
    slotmaps = counts.slotmaps,
    projections = counts.projections,
    dependencies = counts.dependencies,
    c_file = Util.basename(c_path),
  }
end

local function add_pattern_candidate(patterns, l0, ops, stencil_id, selector_sig)
  local pattern_num = selector_key_num(ops)
  local score = sig_pop(selector_sig)
  local list = patterns[pattern_num]
  if not list then list = {}; patterns[pattern_num] = list end
  list[#list + 1] = score * U32 + stencil_id

  if #(ops or {}) == 1 then
    local opcode = OPCODE[op_name(ops[1])] or nil
    if opcode and opcode >= 0 and opcode < 256 then
      if selector_sig == "0x0000000000000000ULL" or not l0[opcode] then l0[opcode] = stencil_id end
    end
  end
end

local function load_pattern_aliases(db, patterns, l0)
  local n = 0
  local sql = [[SELECT u.bank_id AS id, u.selector_sig AS selector_sig, p.ops_json AS ops_json
    FROM unique_forms u JOIN form_patterns p ON p.dedupe_key = u.dedupe_key
    WHERE u.lower_ok = 1 AND u.stencil_json IS NOT NULL AND u.bank_id IS NOT NULL
    ORDER BY p.ops_key, u.id]]
  for row in db:nrows(sql) do
    add_pattern_candidate(patterns, l0, assert(cjson.decode(row.ops_json)), tonumber(row.id), as_c_u64_literal(row.selector_sig))
    n = n + 1
    if n % 100000 == 0 then io.stderr:write(string.format("[bank] loaded %d pattern aliases\n", n)) end
  end
  return n
end

function M.build(bank_src, out_dir)
  bank_src = bank_src or "build/stencil_bank"
  out_dir = out_dir or "build/sponbank"
  mkdir_p(out_dir)

  local db_path = bank_src
  if not tostring(db_path):match("%.sqlite$") then db_path = bank_src .. "/stencil_bank.sqlite" end
  assert(Util.path_exists(db_path), "missing SQLite stencil bank: " .. db_path)
  local db = assert(sqlite3.open(db_path))

  local total_stencils, max_id = 0, 0
  for row in db:nrows("SELECT COUNT(*) AS n, COALESCE(MAX(bank_id), 0) AS max_id FROM unique_forms WHERE lower_ok = 1 AND stencil_json IS NOT NULL AND bank_id IS NOT NULL") do
    total_stencils = tonumber(row.n) or 0
    max_id = tonumber(row.max_id) or 0
  end
  assert(total_stencils > 0, "stencil_bank.sqlite has no lowered stencil rows")

  local rows_per_shard = tonumber(os.getenv("BANK_ROWS_PER_SHARD") or "50000") or 50000
  local patterns = {}
  local l0 = {}
  local shards = {}
  local t0 = os.time()
  local shard_index = 1
  local first_id = 1
  while first_id <= max_id do
    local last_id = math.min(max_id, first_id + rows_per_shard - 1)
    io.stderr:write(string.format("[bank] generating shard %d ids=%d..%d\n", shard_index, first_id, last_id))
    local shard = emit_sqlite_shard(db, out_dir, shard_index, first_id, last_id, patterns, l0)
    if shard.count > 0 then shards[#shards + 1] = shard end
    first_id = last_id + 1
    shard_index = shard_index + 1
  end
  table.sort(shards, function(a, b) return a.first < b.first end)

  io.stderr:write("[bank] loading pattern aliases\n")
  local pattern_aliases = load_pattern_aliases(db, patterns, l0)
  db:close()

  io.stderr:write("[bank] generating index/selector\n")
  local index = emit_index(out_dir, shards, patterns, l0, total_stencils)

  local summary = {
    kind = "sponjit.c_stencil_bank_build.v1",
    source = db_path,
    output = out_dir,
    stencils = total_stencils,
    max_id = max_id,
    rows_per_shard = rows_per_shard,
    chunks = #shards,
    patterns = index.patterns,
    candidates = index.candidates,
    pattern_aliases = pattern_aliases,
    seconds = os.time() - t0,
    shards = shards,
    index = index,
  }
  assert(Util.write_json(out_dir .. "/sponbank_build_manifest.json", summary))
  io.stderr:write(string.format("[bank] stencils=%d patterns=%d candidates=%d shards=%d done in %ds\n", summary.stencils, summary.patterns, summary.candidates, summary.chunks, summary.seconds))
  return summary
end

if arg and arg[0] and tostring(arg[0]):match("build_bank%.lua$") then
  M.build(arg[1] or "build/stencil_bank", arg[2] or "build/sponbank")
end

return M

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# build_stencils.sh — Chunked SpongeJIT foundry metadata build.
#
# Pipeline:
#   grammar enumeration -> weighted JSON chunks -> parallel SSA workers
#   -> per-worker SQLite normal-form DBs -> global normal-form dedupe
#   -> lower each unique supported final form once.
#
# This is a chunked replacement for the monolithic foundry pass. The semantic
# key is the foundry key: the SSA-consumed canonical Stencil IR hash.
# Source opcode sequences and fact contracts are aliases, not the equivalence key.
# Output: build/stencil_bank/stencil_bank.sqlite for src/build_bank.lua.

set -euo pipefail
cd "$(dirname "$0")"

if command -v luarocks >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  eval "$(luarocks --lua-version=5.1 path 2>/dev/null || luarocks path)"
fi
export LUA_PATH LUA_CPATH PATH

log() { printf '[stencils] %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

CORES=$(nproc 2>/dev/null || echo 16)
CHUNKS=${CHUNKS:-128}
WORKERS=${WORKERS:-$CORES}
MAX_ARITY=${MAX_ARITY:-4}
MAX_FACT_COMBOS=${MAX_FACT_COMBOS:-32}
FACT_AXIS_MODE=${FACT_AXIS_MODE:-curated}
WORKER_PROGRESS_SEQS=${WORKER_PROGRESS_SEQS:-1000}
OUT_DIR=${OUT_DIR:-"$PWD/build/stencil_bank"}
SPON_TMP=${SPON_TMP:-"$OUT_DIR"}
KEEP_STENCIL_PARTIALS=${KEEP_STENCIL_PARTIALS:-0}

export CHUNKS WORKERS MAX_ARITY MAX_FACT_COMBOS FACT_AXIS_MODE WORKER_PROGRESS_SEQS SPON_TMP

mkdir -p "$SPON_TMP"

if [ "$KEEP_STENCIL_PARTIALS" != 1 ]; then
  rm -f "$SPON_TMP"/grammar_chunk_*.json \
        "$SPON_TMP"/grammar_chunk_manifest.json \
        "$SPON_TMP"/grammar_worker_*.sqlite \
        "$SPON_TMP"/grammar_forms_*.jsonl \
        "$SPON_TMP"/grammar_result_*.json \
        "$SPON_TMP"/stencil_bank.sqlite \
        "$SPON_TMP"/stencil_bank_manifest.json \
        "$SPON_TMP"/worker_*.log \
        "$SPON_TMP"/build_config.env
fi

cat > "$SPON_TMP/build_config.env" <<EOF
CHUNKS=$CHUNKS
WORKERS=$WORKERS
MAX_ARITY=$MAX_ARITY
MAX_FACT_COMBOS=$MAX_FACT_COMBOS
FACT_AXIS_MODE=$FACT_AXIS_MODE
WORKER_PROGRESS_SEQS=$WORKER_PROGRESS_SEQS
SPON_TMP=$SPON_TMP
EOF

log "config: chunks=$CHUNKS workers=$WORKERS max_arity=$MAX_ARITY fact_mode=$FACT_AXIS_MODE max_fact_combos=$MAX_FACT_COMBOS out=$SPON_TMP"

log "1/3 grammar enumeration + weighted chunk plan"
luajit -e '
package.path = "src/?.lua;src/?/init.lua;" .. package.path
local Util = require("src.util")
local Grammar = require("src.grammar_enum")
local FactAxes = require("src.ssa_fact_axes")

local max_arity = tonumber(os.getenv("MAX_ARITY") or "4") or 4
local chunks = tonumber(os.getenv("CHUNKS") or "128") or 128
local config = {
  max_fact_combos = tonumber(os.getenv("MAX_FACT_COMBOS") or "32") or 32,
  fact_axis_mode = tostring(os.getenv("FACT_AXIS_MODE") or "curated"),
}
local tmp = assert(os.getenv("SPON_TMP"), "SPON_TMP unset")
local function t(name) return tmp .. "/" .. name end

local seqs = Grammar.generate_all(max_arity)

-- generate_all() is handler-deduped. Add exact L0 floor forms for every
-- concrete opcode so the bank keeps total floor coverage explicit.
local seen_l0 = {}
for _, seq in ipairs(seqs) do
  if #(seq.ops or {}) == 1 then
    local op = seq.ops[1]
    local name = type(op) == "table" and op.op or tostring(op)
    seen_l0[name] = true
  end
end
for _, seq in ipairs(Grammar.generate_l0_all()) do
  local op = seq.ops[1]
  local name = type(op) == "table" and op.op or tostring(op)
  if not seen_l0[name] then
    seqs[#seqs + 1] = seq
    seen_l0[name] = true
  end
end

local function estimate_combos(ops)
  local axes = FactAxes.axes_for_ops(ops)
  local subsets = FactAxes.subsets(axes, config)
  return math.max(1, #subsets)
end

local items, total_cost, max_cost = {}, 0, 0
for i, seq in ipairs(seqs) do
  local combos = estimate_combos(seq.ops)
  items[#items + 1] = {idx = i, ops = seq.ops, cost = combos}
  total_cost = total_cost + combos
  if combos > max_cost then max_cost = combos end
  if i % 50000 == 0 then io.stderr:write(string.format("[plan] costed %d/%d sequences\n", i, #seqs)) end
end

table.sort(items, function(a, b)
  if a.cost ~= b.cost then return a.cost > b.cost end
  return a.idx < b.idx
end)

local buckets = {}
for ci = 1, chunks do buckets[ci] = {cost = 0, count = 0, ops = {}} end
for _, item in ipairs(items) do
  local best = 1
  for ci = 2, chunks do if buckets[ci].cost < buckets[best].cost then best = ci end end
  local b = buckets[best]
  b.cost = b.cost + item.cost
  b.count = b.count + 1
  b.ops[#b.ops + 1] = item.ops
end

local min_cost, max_bucket_cost = math.huge, 0
local manifest = {
  max_arity = max_arity,
  chunks = chunks,
  sequences = #seqs,
  estimated_compiles = total_cost,
  max_sequence_combos = max_cost,
  fact_axis_mode = config.fact_axis_mode,
  max_fact_combos = config.max_fact_combos,
  buckets = {},
}
for ci = 1, chunks do
  local b = buckets[ci]
  if b.cost < min_cost then min_cost = b.cost end
  if b.cost > max_bucket_cost then max_bucket_cost = b.cost end
  Util.write_json(t("grammar_chunk_" .. ci .. ".json"), b.ops)
  manifest.buckets[#manifest.buckets + 1] = {chunk = ci, sequences = b.count, estimated_compiles = b.cost}
end
Util.write_json(t("grammar_chunk_manifest.json"), manifest)
io.stderr:write(string.format("[plan] %d seqs incl exact L0, arity<=%d, %d weighted chunks, est_compiles=%d, per_chunk=%d..%d, max_seq_combos=%d\n",
  #seqs, max_arity, chunks, total_cost, min_cost == math.huge and 0 or min_cost, max_bucket_cost, max_cost))
'

log "2/3 SSA + per-worker SQLite ($WORKERS workers over $CHUNKS chunks)"
seq 1 "$CHUNKS" | xargs -P "$WORKERS" -I{} sh -c '
  set -e
  ci="$1"
  log="$SPON_TMP/worker_${ci}.log"
  echo "[worker ${ci}] START" >&2
  luajit src/worker_compile.lua "$ci" >"$log" 2>&1
  tail -n 3 "$log" >&2 || true
  echo "[worker ${ci}] DONE log=$log" >&2
' sh {}

log "3/3 global normal-form dedupe + unique lowering"
luajit src/dedupe_normal_forms.lua "$SPON_TMP" "$CHUNKS" "$SPON_TMP/stencil_bank.sqlite" 2>&1

log "done: $SPON_TMP/stencil_bank.sqlite"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# build_bank.sh — Build libsponbank.so from the current stencil normal-form DB.
#
# This consumes build/stencil_bank/stencil_bank.sqlite produced by
# build_stencils.sh. The SQLite DB already contains global unique SSA-consumed
# normal forms; C generation reads only that DB and does not touch per-worker artifacts.

set -euo pipefail
cd "$(dirname "$0")"

if command -v luarocks >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  eval "$(luarocks --lua-version=5.1 path 2>/dev/null || luarocks path)"
fi
export LUA_PATH LUA_CPATH PATH

log() { printf '[bank.sh] %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

CORES=$(nproc 2>/dev/null || echo 16)
WORKERS=${WORKERS:-$CORES}
BANK_SRC=${BANK_SRC:-"$PWD/build/stencil_bank"}
OUT_DIR=${OUT_DIR:-"$PWD/build/sponbank"}
FORCE_BANK=${FORCE_BANK:-0}

mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

DB="$BANK_SRC/stencil_bank.sqlite"
if [ ! -s "$DB" ]; then
  log "missing SQLite stencil bank: $DB"
  log "run first: cd $PWD && make stencils"
  exit 1
fi

STAMP="$OUT_DIR/.bank.cachekey"
cache_key() {
  {
    printf 'BANK_SRC=%s\n' "$BANK_SRC"
    sha256sum build_bank.sh src/build_bank.lua include/sponbank.h 2>/dev/null || true
    stat -c 'stencil_bank.sqlite %s %Y' "$DB"
  } | sha256sum | awk '{print $1}'
}
KEY=$(cache_key)

if [ "$FORCE_BANK" != 1 ] && [ -s "$OUT_DIR/libsponbank.so" ] && [ -f "$STAMP" ] && [ "$(cat "$STAMP")" = "$KEY" ]; then
  log "cache hit: $OUT_DIR/libsponbank.so"
  exit 0
fi

log "config: workers=$WORKERS bank_src=$BANK_SRC out=$OUT_DIR"

log "1/4 generate C shards + selector"
rm -f "$OUT_DIR"/bank_chunk_*.c "$OUT_DIR"/bank_chunk_*.o "$OUT_DIR"/bank_chunk_*.inc \
      "$OUT_DIR"/bank_index.c "$OUT_DIR"/bank_index.o "$OUT_DIR"/objects.rsp
luajit src/build_bank.lua "$DB" "$OUT_DIR" 2>&1

log "2/4 compile C shards ($WORKERS jobs)"
find "$OUT_DIR" -maxdepth 1 -name 'bank_chunk_*.c' -print | sort -V \
  | xargs -P "$WORKERS" -I{} sh -c '
      set -e
      c="$1"
      o="${c%.c}.o"
      echo "[cc] $(basename "$c")" >&2
      gcc -std=c99 -O2 -fPIC -Iinclude -c "$c" -o "$o"
    ' sh {}

log "3/4 compile index"
gcc -std=c99 -O2 -fPIC -Iinclude -c "$OUT_DIR/bank_index.c" -o "$OUT_DIR/bank_index.o"

log "4/4 link libsponbank.so"
{
  printf '%s\n' "$OUT_DIR/bank_index.o"
  find "$OUT_DIR" -maxdepth 1 -name 'bank_chunk_*.o' -print | sort -V
} > "$OUT_DIR/objects.rsp"
gcc -shared -o "$OUT_DIR/libsponbank.so" @"$OUT_DIR/objects.rsp"
printf '%s\n' "$KEY" > "$STAMP"

log "built: $OUT_DIR/libsponbank.so ($(wc -c < "$OUT_DIR/libsponbank.so" | tr -d ' ') bytes)"
log "exports"
nm -D --defined-only "$OUT_DIR/libsponbank.so" | rg 'spon_' >&2 || true
log "done"

puc_bytecode.lua
puc_proto_dump.c

-- puc_bytecode.lua
-- Static PUC Lua bytecode operand/liveness-ish fact extraction.

local M = {}
local util = require("tools.jit_harness.util")
local FactSchema = require("tools.jit_harness.fact_schema")

local function find_lua_root(repo_root, awfy_root)
    local candidates = {
        awfy_root,
        repo_root and (repo_root .. "/.vendor/Lua"),
        ".vendor/Lua",
    }
    for _, p in ipairs(candidates) do
        if p and util.path_exists(p .. "/liblua.a") and util.path_exists(p .. "/lopcodes.h") then
            return p
        end
    end
    return nil
end

local function build_tool(config, name, source_file)
    config = config or {}
    local repo_root = util.abspath(config.repo_root or util.find_repo_root(".") or ".")
    local lua_root = util.abspath(find_lua_root(repo_root, config.lua_root or config.awfy_root) or (repo_root .. "/.vendor/Lua"))
    local out = config[name .. "_path"] or config.tool_path or (repo_root .. "/experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/build/tools/" .. name)
    if util.path_exists(out) then return out end
    util.mkdir_p(util.dirname(out))
    local src = repo_root .. "/experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/tools/jit_harness/" .. source_file
    local cmd = string.format("cc -I%s %s %s -lm -ldl -o %s",
        util.shell_quote(lua_root), util.shell_quote(src), util.shell_quote(lua_root .. "/liblua.a"), util.shell_quote(out))
    local ok, text = util.run_capture(cmd)
    if not ok then return nil, text end
    return out
end

function M.ensure_dump_tool(config)
    return build_tool(config, "puc_proto_dump", "puc_proto_dump.c")
end

function M.ensure_trace_tool(config)
    return build_tool(config, "puc_trace_operands", "puc_trace_operands.c")
end

local function read_dump(path)
    local rows = {}
    local text = util.read_file(path)
    if not text then return rows end
    local header = true
    for line in text:gmatch("[^\n]+") do
        if header then header = false else
            local c = {}
            for col in (line .. "\t"):gmatch("([^\t]*)\t") do c[#c+1] = col end
            if #c >= 13 then
                rows[#rows+1] = {
                    proto = c[1], depth = tonumber(c[2]), pc = tonumber(c[3]), opcode = tonumber(c[4]), name = c[5],
                    a = tonumber(c[6]), b = tonumber(c[7]), c = tonumber(c[8]), k = tonumber(c[9]),
                    bx = tonumber(c[10]), sbx = tonumber(c[11]), ax = tonumber(c[12]), word = tonumber(c[13]),
                }
            end
        end
    end
    return rows
end

function M.dump_file(path, config)
    local tool, err = M.ensure_dump_tool(config)
    if not tool then return nil, err end
    local cmd = util.shell_quote(tool) .. " " .. util.shell_quote(path)
    local ok, out = util.run_capture(cmd)
    if not ok then return nil, out end
    local tmp = os.tmpname()
    util.write_file(tmp, out)
    local rows = read_dump(tmp)
    os.remove(tmp)
    return rows
end

local function op_key(a, b)
    return a.name .. "|" .. b.name
end

local function fact_key_for_rewrite(kind, ops)
    if kind == "move_move_forward" then
        return "MOVE:move_def;MOVE:move_uses_previous_def"
    elseif kind == "move_move_empty" then
        return "MOVE:redundant_move;MOVE:redundant_move"
    elseif kind == "load_move_final_dst" then
        return ops[1] .. ":load_def;MOVE:move_uses_previous_def"
    elseif kind == "op_move_final_dst" then
        return ops[1] .. ":i64;MOVE:move_uses_previous_def"
    elseif kind == "op_return1" then
        return ops[1] .. ":i64;RETURN1:returns_previous_def"
    end
end

local pure_arith = { ADD=true, SUB=true, MUL=true, ADDI=true }
local pure_load = { LOADI=true, LOADF=true, LOADK=true }

local function classify_pair(a, b)
    local ops = { a.name, b.name }
    if a.name == "MOVE" and b.name == "MOVE" then
        if a.a == a.b and b.a == b.b then return "move_move_empty", ops end
        if a.a == b.b then return "move_move_forward", ops end
    end
    if pure_load[a.name] and b.name == "MOVE" and a.a == b.b then
        return "load_move_final_dst", ops
    end
    if pure_arith[a.name] and b.name == "MOVE" and a.a == b.b then
        return "op_move_final_dst", ops
    end
    if pure_arith[a.name] and b.name == "RETURN1" and a.a == b.a then
        return "op_return1", ops
    end
    return nil, ops
end

local function parse_rows_text(text)
    local rows = {}
    if not text then return rows end
    local header = true
    for line in text:gmatch("[^\n]+") do
        if header then header = false else
            local c = {}
            for col in (line .. "\t"):gmatch("([^\t]*)\t") do c[#c+1] = col end
            if #c >= 13 then
                rows[#rows+1] = {
                    seq = tonumber(c[1]), proto = c[2], pc = tonumber(c[3]), opcode = tonumber(c[4]), name = c[5],
                    a = tonumber(c[6]), b = tonumber(c[7]), c = tonumber(c[8]), k = tonumber(c[9]),
                    bx = tonumber(c[10]), sbx = tonumber(c[11]), ax = tonumber(c[12]), word = tonumber(c[13]),
                }
            end
        end
    end
    return rows
end

function M.trace_file(path, output_path, config)
    config = config or {}
    local tool, err = M.ensure_trace_tool(config)
    if not tool then return nil, err end
    local limit = tonumber(config.trace_limit or 100000) or 100000
    if output_path then util.mkdir_p(util.dirname(output_path)) end
    local parts = { util.shell_quote(tool), util.shell_quote(path), tostring(limit) }
    if output_path then parts[#parts + 1] = util.shell_quote(output_path) end
    local cmd = table.concat(parts, " ")
    if config.timeout_seconds then
        cmd = "timeout " .. tostring(tonumber(config.timeout_seconds) or 5) .. "s " .. cmd
    end
    if output_path == nil then
        local ok, out = util.run_capture(cmd)
        local rows = parse_rows_text(out)
        if #rows > 0 then return rows, out end
        if not ok then return nil, out end
        return rows, out
    end
    local ok, out = util.run_capture(cmd)
    if util.path_exists(output_path) then
        local rows = parse_rows_text(util.read_file(output_path))
        if #rows > 0 then return rows, out end
    end
    if not ok then return nil, out end
    return {}, out
end

local function accumulate_rewrite_facts_from_rows(rows, result, use_dynamic)
    for i = 1, #rows - 1 do
        local a, b = rows[i], rows[i + 1]
        if a.proto == b.proto and a.pc + 1 == b.pc then
            local key = op_key(a, b)
            if use_dynamic then
                result.dynamic_window_counts[key] = (result.dynamic_window_counts[key] or 0) + 1
            else
                result.static_window_counts[key] = (result.static_window_counts[key] or 0) + 1
            end
            local rkind, ops = classify_pair(a, b)
            if rkind then
                local fk = fact_key_for_rewrite(rkind, ops)
                local full = key .. " @ " .. fk
                local target = use_dynamic and result.rewrite_fact_counts or result.rewrite_fact_static_counts
                target[full] = (target[full] or 0) + 1
                result.operand_shape_counts[full] = (result.operand_shape_counts[full] or 0) + 1
            end
        end
    end
end

function M.profile_dynamic_files(files, config)
    config = config or {}
    local result = {
        files = 0,
        instructions = 0,
        dynamic_window_counts = {},
        operand_shape_counts = {},
        rewrite_fact_counts = {},
        rejects = {},
        trace_dir = config.trace_dir,
    }
    if config.trace_dir then util.mkdir_p(config.trace_dir) end
    local processed = 0
    local include = config.include
    print(string.format("[puc-dyn] tracing operands for up to %d/%d files", tonumber(config.max_files or #(files or {})), #(files or {})))
    for idx, file in ipairs(files or {}) do
        if config.max_files and processed >= config.max_files then break end
        local path = file.path or file
        if include and not tostring(path):find(include, 1, true) then
            -- skip
        else
            print(string.format("[puc-dyn] %d/%d %s", idx, #(files or {}), tostring(path)))
            local out = config.trace_dir and (config.trace_dir .. "/" .. util.basename(path):gsub("%.lua$", "") .. ".trace_operands.tsv") or nil
            local rows, err = M.trace_file(path, out, config)
            if not rows then
                print("[puc-dyn] reject: " .. tostring(err):sub(1, 160))
                result.rejects[#result.rejects+1] = { path = path, error = err }
            else
                processed = processed + 1
                result.files = result.files + 1
                result.instructions = result.instructions + #rows
                print(string.format("[puc-dyn] traced %d instructions", #rows))
                accumulate_rewrite_facts_from_rows(rows, result, true)
            end
        end
    end
    return result
end

function M.profile_files(files, config, dynamic_profile)
    config = config or {}
    local result = {
        files = 0,
        protos = 0,
        instructions = 0,
        static_window_counts = {},
        operand_shape_counts = {},
        rewrite_fact_counts = {},
        rewrite_fact_static_counts = {},
        rejects = {},
    }

    print(string.format("[puc-static] dumping operands for %d files", #(files or {})))
    for idx, file in ipairs(files or {}) do
        local fpath = file.path or file
        if idx == 1 or idx % 5 == 0 or idx == #(files or {}) then
            print(string.format("[puc-static] %d/%d %s", idx, #(files or {}), tostring(fpath)))
        end
        local rows, err = M.dump_file(fpath, config)
        if not rows then
            print("[puc-static] reject: " .. tostring(err):sub(1, 160))
            result.rejects[#result.rejects+1] = { path = fpath, error = err }
        else
            result.files = result.files + 1
            result.instructions = result.instructions + #rows
            local by_proto = {}
            for _, ins in ipairs(rows) do
                by_proto[ins.proto] = by_proto[ins.proto] or {}
                table.insert(by_proto[ins.proto], ins)
            end
            for _, proto_rows in pairs(by_proto) do
                result.protos = result.protos + 1
                table.sort(proto_rows, function(a, b) return a.pc < b.pc end)

# SponJIT Copy-Link-Patch Native Stencil ABI Specification

**Status:** draft specification; not implemented.

**Spec version:** 0.1

**Decision:** Copy-Link-Patch uses a **Native Stencil ABI with Continuation
Relocations**. C-function-shaped stencils are not linkable stencils.

The keywords **MUST**, **MUST NOT**, **SHOULD**, **SHOULD NOT**, and **MAY** are
normative.

---

## 1. Scope

This specification defines the ABI for SpongeJIT Copy-Link-Patch stencils.

It specifies:

- native stencil descriptors
- physical endpoint contracts
- data relocations
- control relocations
- continuation endpoints
- projection recipes
- validation rules
- image linking rules
- rejection conditions

It does not specify:

- a general machine-code assembler
- a full optimizing compiler
- a Lalin/Cranelift backend
- payload lease policy for all future object-model facts
- final binary encoding of descriptor arrays

---

## 2. Unified tier model

There is one target stencil ABI: native stencils.

C-function-shaped tiles are an old implementation artifact, not a second target ABI.
A conforming SpongeJIT bank MUST expose native stencils for both floor coverage and
hot fused images.

### 2.1 Floor / Tier 1 image

The floor/Tier 1 image uses the same native stencil ABI as every hotter image.

Tier 1 behavior is the simplest legal linking policy:

```text
select stencil templates -> canonical slot endpoints -> copy bytes -> patch data/control relocations -> enter image
```

Tier 1 SHOULD keep boundaries frame-synchronized unless projection recipes prove a
more direct endpoint is valid.

Tier 1 MUST NOT require a C-function return boundary for normal success flow.

### 2.2 Hot fused image

A hot fused image uses the same stencil descriptors and materializer, but is chosen by the Tier 2 online fusion planner. The planner may select longer spans, choose stencil variants, preserve register endpoints across stencils, insert or omit bridge stencils, prove seam removal, skip redundant guards through fact flow, and use richer projection recipes.

Hot fused image behavior is:

```text
observe stable hot span
  -> online fusion planner chooses/proves a FusionPlan from bank stencils
    -> materializer lays out one image
      -> patch data/control relocations
        -> enter once
```

A hot fused image MAY cover many more atoms than any single precomputed stencil. The atoms may come from L1, L2, L3, or later bank layers; the layer does not matter to Tier 2. A 4000-atom hot span can be materialized as one linked image by composing many atom instances. Atom arity/layer limits the size of individual templates, not the length of the fused image.

The initial bank target is local arity <= 4 with roughly 32 useful fact combinations per atom shape after dedupe/ranking. This is a vocabulary target, not a trace-length target. Tier 2 obtains long-range optimization by composing many such atoms and choosing variants/endpoints/projections across the whole fused span.

No tier MAY synthesize arbitrary machine instructions at runtime.

All tiers MAY copy, lay out, link, and patch only descriptors emitted by the foundry. The online fusion planner may optimize the stencil graph; the linker/materializer only realizes that chosen graph mechanically.

---

## 3. Terms

### Stencil

A **stencil** is a relocatable native code region with explicit entry and exit
endpoints.

A stencil is not a C function.

### Endpoint

An **endpoint** is a named control position inside a stencil:

- `ENTRY`
- `OK`
- `GUARD_FAIL`
- `RESIDUAL`
- `BOUNDARY`
- `RETURN`
- `BRANCH_TRUE`
- `BRANCH_FALSE`

### Data relocation

A **data relocation** patches a value consumed by native code: slot offset,
immediate, constant, helper address, dependency address, payload address, etc.

### Control relocation

A **control relocation** patches a control-transfer target: success continuation,
guard failure target, residual target, branch target, projection stub, return target.

### Projection

A **projection** reconstructs interpreter-visible state at a non-success exit.

### Physical ABI

A **physical ABI** defines architecture, platform calling convention, fixed register
roles, clobber rules, stack rules, and context/frame locations.

---

## 4. Non-goals and prohibitions

A Copy-Link-Patch implementation MUST NOT treat existing `exit`/`fail` metadata
holes as native branch targets unless they are retyped as control relocations.

A Copy-Link-Patch implementation MUST NOT delete a frame store across an exit unless
that exit has a complete projection recipe.

A Copy-Link-Patch implementation MUST NOT patch unsupported hole roles with dummy
values.

A Copy-Link-Patch implementation MUST NOT infer clobbers or endpoint locations by
reverse-engineering machine code.

A Copy-Link-Patch implementation MUST NOT require runtime instruction encoding for
ordinary stencil linking.

---

## 5. Descriptor model

A stencil catalog MUST provide these descriptor classes:

```text
SponPhysicalAbiDesc
SponStencilDesc
SponEndpointDesc
SponLocationDesc
SponDataRelocDesc
SponControlRelocDesc
SponFactTransferDesc
SponEffectDesc
SponProjectionDesc
SponProjectionEntryDesc
SponDependencyDesc
```

The binary representation is implementation-defined, but the semantic fields in
this specification are mandatory unless explicitly marked optional.


[749 more lines in file. Use offset=171 to continue.]

required_sig:  u64
  checked_sig:   u64
  produced_sig:  u64
  killed_sig:    u64
```

Rules:

Before linking a stencil at a span, the linker MUST verify:

```text
(required_sig & ~available_facts) == 0
```

After a stencil succeeds, the linker/runtime fact model MUST apply:

```text
facts_out = (facts_in & ~killed_sig) | produced_sig | checked_sig
```

Payload facts not represented in the 64-bit signature MUST be represented by
separate dependency/payload descriptors before materialization.

A stencil requiring an unavailable payload MUST be rejected.

---

## 12. Effect descriptor

```text
SponEffectDesc:
  may_frame_read:       bool
  may_frame_write:      bool
  may_heap_read:        bool
  may_heap_write:       bool
  may_call:             bool
  may_gc:               bool
  may_throw:            bool
  may_yield:            bool
  may_barrier:          bool
  may_debug_observe:    bool
  is_branch:            bool
  is_return:            bool
  is_residual:          bool
```

Rules:

The linker MUST NOT reorder stencils across effects unless the relevant effect
ordering is proven safe by metadata.

For initial Copy-Link-Patch validation, the linker SHOULD reject stencils with
`may_call`, `may_gc`, `may_throw`, or `may_yield` unless fallback/projection behavior
is fully specified.

---

## 13. Projection descriptor

### 13.1 Projection classes

```text
PROJ_SYNCED_FRAME
PROJ_REGISTER
PROJ_VIRTUAL
```

### 13.2 Projection descriptor

```text
SponProjectionDesc:
  projection_id:     u32
  class:             ProjectionClass
  resume_pc:         u32
  resume_op_idx:     u16
  entry_start:       u32
  entry_count:       u16
  flags:             u32
```

### 13.3 Projection entry descriptor

```text
SponProjectionEntryDesc:
  projection_id:     u32
  dst_slot:          u16
  kind:              ProjectionEntryKind
  src0:              Location
  src1:              Location
  aux:               i64
  type:              ValueType
```

Projection entry kinds:

```text
PROJ_ENTRY_FROM_SLOT
PROJ_ENTRY_FROM_REGISTER
PROJ_ENTRY_CONST
PROJ_ENTRY_BOX_I64
PROJ_ENTRY_UNBOX_I64
PROJ_ENTRY_ADD_I64
PROJ_ENTRY_COPY_TVALUE
```

### 13.4 Rules

Every non-success endpoint that can leave the fused image MUST have a projection
descriptor.

A projection descriptor MUST reconstruct every interpreter-visible slot required at
`resume_pc`.

`PROJ_VIRTUAL` entries MUST be explicit side-effect-free expression DAGs.

Projection expressions MUST NOT read heap state unless the projection descriptor
explicitly declares and validates the dependency.

If any source location required by a projection is clobbered before the endpoint,
the image MUST be rejected.

If `resume_pc` or `resume_op_idx` cannot be mapped to the interpreter/floor resume
protocol, the image MUST be rejected.

---

## 14. Dependency descriptor

```text
SponDependencyDesc:

[320 more lines in file. Use offset=600 to continue.]

## 16. Fusion planner and linker algorithm

The online fusion planner is the proof-producing optimization phase. It chooses the stencil instances, variants, bridges, success edges, projection recipes, and seam-removal strategy from the bank vocabulary.

The linker/materializer is the proof-consuming mechanical phase. It MUST NOT invent new machine code, infer projection recipes, delete stores/guards, or perform semantic optimization not present in the `FusionPlan`.

The combined Tier 2 pipeline MUST perform these phases in order or prove an equivalent ordering:

```text
1. Build candidate stencil set from the bank.
2. Online fusion planner selects/proves a FusionPlan.
3. Verify physical ABI compatibility.
4. Resolve canonical slots to actual frame slots.
5. Validate required facts and payload dependencies.
6. Validate endpoint input/output compatibility.
7. Insert only bridge stencils selected by the FusionPlan.
8. Validate clobbers against live endpoint/projection values.
9. Validate projection completeness for all non-success exits.
10. Assign final layout offsets.
11. Resolve control targets from explicit plan edges.
12. Allocate writable image memory.
13. Copy stencil text.
14. Apply data relocations.
15. Apply control relocations.
16. Install projection/fallback stubs.
17. Change memory permissions to executable.
18. Publish image atomically.
```

At any failed validation or materialization step, the linker MUST reject the candidate image and leave the currently active valid image unchanged.

---

## 17. Endpoint compatibility

An edge from producer endpoint `P` to consumer endpoint `C` is compatible iff:

```text
P.output_count satisfies C.input_count
for each required C input:
  there exists a live P output or prior live value with same ValueType and Location
required facts of C are available after P fact transfer
P clobbers do not destroy values needed by C or by exit projections
```

If not compatible, the linker MAY insert bridge stencils.

A bridge stencil MUST itself satisfy this specification.

If no legal bridge sequence is chosen by the fusion plan, the image MUST be rejected.

---

## 18. Layout rules

The layout SHOULD place hot success paths contiguously.

The layout SHOULD place guard/residual/projection/fallback stubs out of line.

If an `OK` edge is fallthrough-capable and the successor `ENTRY` is placed
immediately after the producer body, the linker MAY omit or neutralize the `OK`
control relocation, if the stencil descriptor permits this.

The linker MUST NOT assume fallthrough unless the descriptor marks the endpoint as
fallthrough-capable.

---

## 19. Runtime exit protocol

A non-success endpoint MUST transfer to one of:

```text
projection stub
fallback stub
lower-image resume trampoline
floor/interpreter resume trampoline
VM return trampoline
```

The exit protocol MUST provide:

```text
exit kind
resume pc
resume op index when applicable
projected frame state
fact delta or contradicted fact when applicable
```

The exit protocol MUST be compatible with lower-image, floor, or interpreter
resumption.

---

## 20. Maintained implementation mapping

The maintained implementation currently maps only to abstract native-stencil metadata:

```text
SSA / Stencil IR              -> semantic source for stencil descriptors
AbstractStencilTemplate      -> metadata-only; not executable
SponStencilDesc-shaped ABI   -> lowered metadata view; provisional wire shape
DataReloc / ControlReloc      -> separated relocation classes
Endpoint / Projection metadata-> required for future native materialization
```

There is no maintained executable stencil bank or runtime linker today. Any old callable-function tile ABI is retired and non-conforming. The target architecture is native stencils plus a proof-consuming Copy-Link-Patch materializer, not a dual ABI.

---

## 21. Initial conformance surface

The first conforming stencil set SHOULD be limited to:

```text
i64 slot loads
i64 slot stores
i64 guards
i64 add/sub/mul-like arithmetic
immediates/constants
simple success continuations
guard failure exits with synced-frame or register projection
```

The first conforming stencil set SHOULD NOT include:

```text
shape payloads
field payloads
array payloads
call target payloads
barrier/dependency cells
may_call/may_gc/may_yield stencils
```

This is not a reduced ABI. It is the first conformance surface for validating the
full ABI rules without unresolved payload machinery.

---

## 22. Required validation experiments

### 22.1 Metadata sufficiency

For each candidate stencil class, verify that descriptors can name:

```text
entry endpoint
success endpoint
failure endpoints
inputs
outputs
clobbers
data relocations
control relocations
fact transfer
effects
projection recipes
dependencies
```

### 22.2 Linkability

Given representative i64 stencil sequences, verify:

```text
endpoint matches
bridge insertions
spill insertions
control target resolution
fallthrough eligibility
rejection reasons
```

### 22.3 Projection correctness

For every non-success endpoint, verify:

```text
resume pc correctness
resume op index correctness
all required slots reconstructed
projection source values live
projection expressions explicit and side-effect-free
```

### 22.4 Execution equivalence

For every executable linked image, compare against:

```text
PUC interpreter behavior
floor/native-stencil image behavior
expected frame state
expected exit state
expected resume pc/op index
```

---

## 23. Conformance checklist

A stencil catalog conforms to this specification iff:

```text
[ ] every stencil has a valid physical ABI descriptor
[ ] every stencil has explicit entry metadata
[ ] every control transfer target is represented by endpoint/control-reloc metadata
[ ] every data patch site is represented by data-reloc metadata
[ ] every stencil declares inputs, outputs, and clobbers
[ ] every stencil declares fact transfer
[ ] every stencil declares effects
[ ] every non-success exit has a projection descriptor
[ ] every dependency is explicit and runtime-validatable
[ ] unsupported holes/dependencies reject image construction
[ ] no C-function return boundary is required for hot success flow
```

An online fusion planner conforms iff:

```text
[ ] it chooses only stencils, variants, and bridges present in the bank
[ ] it validates facts and dependencies before selecting a stencil instance
[ ] it validates endpoint compatibility before creating a success edge
[ ] it proves any store/load, box/unbox, or redundant-guard seam it removes
[ ] it assigns every non-success endpoint a complete projection obligation
[ ] it emits explicit plan edges; no semantic edge is implicit fallthrough
[ ] it records typed rejection/deferral reasons
```

A runtime linker/materializer conforms iff:

```text
[ ] it consumes a FusionPlan rather than inventing semantic optimizations
[ ] it validates physical ABI compatibility
[ ] it resolves canonical slots
[ ] it validates clobbers against live values/projections
[ ] it validates projection completeness remains true after layout
[ ] it patches data relocations
[ ] it patches control relocations from explicit plan edges
[ ] it publishes only fully patched executable images
[ ] it leaves the current valid image active on rejection
```

---

## 24. One-line rule

```text
A function stencil is a sealed callable tile.
A native stencil stencil is a linkable region with explicit continuations.
A Tier 2 fusion plan is the proof that those continuations compose.
```

Copy-Link-Patch requires native stencil stencils and a proof-consuming materializer.

# Lalin Lua VM Contract / ABI

This document records the pre-SponJIT contract for the Lalin-native Lua 5.5 VM. It is an architectural gate: SponJIT must consume this contract only after the VM validator, frame/cache rules, native boundary, allocator boundary, and error/yield protocols are implemented and tested.

## Scope

- The VM targets Lua 5.5 semantics in Lalin-native data/control structures.
- PUC Lua is a semantic reference only. PUC layouts, `longjmp`, C-stack behavior, allocator conventions, and internal bytecode/runtime shapes MUST NOT be treated as implementation dependencies.
- Scratch-memory FFI tests are test fixtures only. They are not the stable host ABI.
- SponJIT remains separate and MUST NOT optimize or depend on scaffolding behavior.

## Internal ABI

The internal ABI is the typed product/control contract used by VM regions:

- `Value` MUST remain the canonical tagged value representation.
- `Proto` MUST describe validated bytecode and immutable prototype metadata.
- `Frame` MUST carry explicit call/return metadata, including `result_base`, `call_top`, `resume_mode`, and yieldability flags.
- `LuaThread` MUST carry explicit coroutine/error state, including status, protected-frame head, error value, yieldability counters, and last error code.
- `GlobalState` MUST own VM-wide runtime state and ABI version fields.

Internal pointers carry no implicit nullability or lifetime guarantees. Every non-null/lifetime assumption MUST be enforced by construction, validation, or explicit checks at the boundary where it matters.

## Host ABI

Host-visible access is through sealed API functions only. Host callers MUST NOT rely on internal struct allocation conventions beyond documented ABI fields and version checks. Any host operation that may allocate, call Lua/native code, yield, or error MUST expose that outcome explicitly; it MUST NOT hide VM control flow in a C return convention.

## Native ABI

Native Lua-callable functions use a Lalin-owned ABI contract:

- Native descriptors MUST carry an ABI version.
- Native execution MUST expose normal return, Lua error, yield, OOM, and stack-growth outcomes explicitly.
- Native functions MUST NOT use host exceptions, PUC-style `longjmp`, hidden scheduler behavior, or raw allocator side channels as VM semantics.
- Until native invocation is implemented, native-call paths MUST fail loudly with explicit error state.

## Bytecode Validation Contract

`validate_proto` is the trust boundary for the interpreter and any future JIT.

The validator MUST enforce every assumption used by dispatch and opcode handlers, including:

- opcode range
- register bounds
- constant and child-prototype bounds
- jump target bounds
- paired-instruction invariants such as `LOADKX`/`EXTRAARG` and arithmetic/`MMBIN*`
- call/return register windows
- loop register windows

A JIT MUST NOT consume bytecode accepted only by ad-hoc test fixtures.

## Frame and Cache Invariant

The VM loop may cache `code` and `constants` for the current frame. Whenever the current `Frame*` changes, cached prototype-derived pointers MUST be reloaded from that frame's closure. Cached pointers from a child frame MUST NOT be used to resume a parent.

## Call and Return Invariant

Call result placement is explicit:

- A call's destination is `Frame.result_base` (the caller's `base + A` or preserved tailcall destination).
- Return adjustment MUST NOT substitute `parent.base` for the call result base.
- Child-frame return metadata owns the return-mode payload; parent-frame state owns resumed execution.

## Error and Protected-Unwind Invariant

Opcode/runtime errors MUST flow through explicit error-object/protected-unwind machinery before reaching the outer VM error continuation. The VM MUST preserve `LuaThread.err_value` and `LuaThread.last_error_code` on error. Protected calls and to-be-closed variables MUST NOT depend on host stack unwinding.

## Coroutine and Yield Invariant

Yieldability is VM data, not hidden control flow. Every yieldable path (Lua call, native call, metamethod, iterator, `__close`, protected call) MUST carry enough explicit saved state to resume or reject the yield deterministically.

## Allocator and Extern Boundary

Allocation is VM semantics. Allocation paths MUST use an explicit allocator protocol with `ok`, `step_required`, and `oom` outcomes. The VM MUST NOT embed raw libc/malloc behavior or treat null pointer returns as the only error channel. GC barriers and lifetime ownership are part of the VM contract.

## SponJIT Gate

SponJIT integration is not allowed until these gates are tested:

1. bytecode validator is complete for interpreter/JIT assumptions;
2. frame-cache reload on frame switch is verified;
3. explicit call result base is verified;
4. unified error/protected-unwind path is verified;
5. explicit native ABI boundary is versioned and fail-loud;
6. allocator boundary is explicit and does not hide libc behavior.

content.text:
# SponJIT Tier 2 Online Fusion Planner — Explicit Design Specification

**Workflow:** `wf-sponjit-copy-link-patch`  
**Methodology:** `explicit_programming.md`  
**Status:** design specification; online stencil-graph fusion planner, execution deferred until native stencils exist  
**Supersedes:** the earlier semantic-planner sketch by making the data tree, control tree, and Tier 2 optimization boundary explicit.

---

## 0. One-sentence purpose

SponJIT Tier 2 analyzes a hot span/trace as a stencil graph, selects the best legal cover from prebuilt stencil variants and bridges, validates facts/endpoints/projections/seams, and produces a proof-bearing fusion plan for Copy-Link-Patch materialization without synthesizing arbitrary machine code.

That sentence disciplines the rest of this document:

- if a type does not help select, fuse, validate, or report Tier 2 stencil composition, it does not belong in the planner;
- if an operation performs byte copying, relocation writes, executable-memory permission changes, or publication, it belongs in the materializer/runtime phase;
- if a value is needed only after native code exists, it is future native-linker data, not planner source data;
- if an optimization cannot be expressed as choosing/linking prebuilt stencils, variants, bridges, endpoints, or projection stubs, it is outside Tier 2.

---

## 1. Design stance

This spec applies the explicit-programming rule:

> every distinction that matters to behavior must be represented as a typed value, a named transition, or a declared protocol.

For SponJIT Tier 2, the dangerous hidden distinctions are:

- abstract stencil vs executable native stencil;
- semantic compatibility vs physical register compatibility;
- data relocation vs control relocation;
- projection presence vs projection sufficiency;
- synced-frame safety vs reconstructing projection power;
- missing facts vs unsupported payloads vs dependency failures;
- planner rejection vs planner deferral;
- runtime image materialization vs semantic planning;
- stencil-graph optimization vs arbitrary runtime code generation;
- proof-producing fusion planning vs proof-consuming mechanical linking.

The design below makes those distinctions structural.

---

## 2. Current factual baseline

The maintained SpongeJIT path currently is:

```text
opcode sequence + fact bundle
→ ssa_lift.lua / ssa_ir.lua
→ ssa_opt.lua
→ ssa_to_stencil.lua / stencil_ir.lua
→ stencil_normalize.lua
→ stencil_to_stencil.lua
→ stencil_ir.lua ABI-lowered metadata
→ worker_compile.lua per-worker SSA normal-form SQLite artifacts
→ dedupe_normal_forms.lua global normal-form dedupe + unique lowering
```

Current chunked artifacts:

```text
grammar_chunk_N.json
grammar_worker_N.sqlite
stencil_bank.sqlite  -- unique normal forms, lowered stencils, pattern aliases
```

Removed/retired artifacts:

```text
grammar_c_code_N.c
grammar_holes_N.json
C-function stencil/tile bank
PUC executable runtime integration
```

Current stencils are **abstract metadata**:

```lua
layout = {
  mode = "abstract_stencil",
  executable = false,
  code_offsets = "abstract_zero",
}
```

The planner must preserve this invariant. An abstract stencil is not failed native code. It is a different phase product.

---

## 3. Phase tree

The system is a chain of immutable phase products. Derived data may be cached at phase boundaries, but must not become source truth for earlier phases.

| Phase | Source input | Product | Current? | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Fact/opcode enumeration | bytecode windows, fact bundles | `SemanticOpStream`, `FactInputSet` | yes | Foundry-facing input. |
| SSA | semantic ops + facts | `SsaResult` | yes | Preserves source pc, memory tokens, exits, value residencies. |
| Stencil IR | SSA result | `StencilShape` | yes | Semantic, hole-parametric shape. `exit`/`fail` are not data holes. |
| Abstract stencil | Stencil + contract | `AbstractStencilTemplate` | yes | Metadata-only descriptor, no executable offsets. |
| Tier 2 online fusion planner | stencil catalog + planning/fusion request | `FusionPlan` or `RejectionReport` | this spec | Stencil-graph optimization, semantic linking, fact flow, projections, seam legality. |
| Native stencil bank | native assembler/emitter output | `NativeStencilTemplate` | future | Real bytes, concrete relocs, endpoint offsets. |
| Runtime materializer | native bank + fusion plan + facts | `LinkedImage` | future | Mechanical copy/link/patch/publish of the chosen plan. |

The planner is intentionally between abstract stencils and native images. In the current implementation era it runs over abstract descriptors to prove the design. In the future runtime era the same planning protocol runs over proof-bearing native stencil templates before materialization.

### 3.1 Atom arity is not FusionPlan length

A precomputed stencil template at the Tier 2 input boundary is an **atom** from the bank vocabulary. It may be an L1, L2, L3, or later-layer atom. The layer does not matter to Tier 2: Tier 2 performs long-range fusion over the atom stream it is given.

```text
hot span length = 4000 atoms
FusionPlan length = enough atom instances to cover those 4000 atoms
```

Tier 2 optimization comes from analyzing the long composed atom graph: direct continuations, seam legality, fact flow, projection obligations, guard redundancy, bridge choices, and layout. The arity/layer of one atom is not the max optimization range.

### 3.2 Initial atom vocabulary target

The working saturation target is:

```text
local atom arity <= 4
approximately 32 useful fact combinations per atom shape
```

This is enough material for Tier 2 to test the thesis because the planner composes many atom instances into one `FusionPlan`. The 32 fact combinations are local specialization choices; the long trace specialization emerges from the product of choices across the atom stream.

The planner must therefore optimize across atom boundaries rather than ask the foundry for giant precomputed traces. The useful questions are:

```text
which atom variant fits this fact state?
which endpoint shape keeps values live?
which guard is redundant after previous facts?
which store/load or box/unbox seam is removable?
which projection repairs the frame if we exit before resync?
```

If atom descriptors do not expose those answers, increasing arity or fact-combo count will not fix Tier 2. The limiting factor is endpoint/projection/fact-contract quality, not raw atom arity.

---

## 4. Source data vs derived data

### 4.1 Planner source data

The planner may treat these as source for its phase:

```text
PlanningRequest
FusionRequest                  -- hot span/trace plus policy and observed facts
StencilCatalog
AbstractStencilTemplate       -- current design/proof phase
NativeStencilTemplate         -- future runtime phase, only if NativeBody exists
FactState
FrameState
RuntimeObservationSample       -- optional corpus/profile input
VmGateState                    -- tells planner what integration is forbidden
```

### 4.2 Planner-derived data

The planner computes these:

```text
StencilCandidate
VariantChoice
BridgeChoice
StencilInstance
PlanEdge
FactTrace
ProjectionObligation
SeamCandidate
SeamLegalityResult
FusionPlan
MaterializationPlanSeed
PlannerReport
RejectionReport
```

### 4.3 Not planner source data

These are derived summaries or future-native fields. The planner may inspect them, but must not mistake them for semantic truth:

```text
stencil_hash
pattern_key
numeric ABI enum fields
flattened start/count array ranges
abstract_zero code offsets
reloc_kind = 0 on abstract stencils
wire flags such as ABSTRACT or NATIVE
future executable addresses
future branch deltas
```

---

## 5. Data tree

This section is the semantic model. Lua may implement these as tagged tables, and C may lower them to enums/arrays, but those encodings are not the design root.

### 5.1 Planning request

```text
PlanningRequest = {
  region_id: RegionId,
  pc_start: SourcePc,
  pc_end: SourcePc,
  opcode_stream: SemanticOpStream,
  entry_facts: FactState,
  observed_facts: FactState,
  frame_state: FrameState,
  policy: PlanningPolicy,
  vm_gates: VmGateState,
}
```

SPONJIT_TIER2_PLANNER_SPEC.md-1158- 
SPONJIT_TIER2_PLANNER_SPEC.md-1159- ```text
SPONJIT_TIER2_PLANNER_SPEC.md-1164- 
SPONJIT_TIER2_PLANNER_SPEC.md-1165- - find store/load, box/unbox, and redundant-guard seam candidates;
SPONJIT_TIER2_PLANNER_SPEC.md:1166: - produce `SeamLegalityResult` values;
SPONJIT_TIER2_PLANNER_SPEC.md-1167- - report blockers rather than silently declining optimization.
SPONJIT_TIER2_PLANNER_SPEC.md-1168-

The planner currently creates semantic control edges. Future native materialization turns compatible `ControlReloc` values into branch patches.

### 5.11 Plan edge

```text
PlanEdge =
  SuccessEdge(from: EndpointRef, to: EndpointRef, compatibility: EdgeCompatibility)
| GuardExitEdge(from: EndpointRef, projection: ProjectionRecipe, resume: ResumeTarget)
| ResidualExitEdge(from: EndpointRef, projection: ProjectionRecipe, resume: ResumeTarget)
| BoundaryExitEdge(from: EndpointRef, projection: ProjectionRecipe, resume: ResumeTarget)
| UnloweredExitEdge(from: EndpointRef, projection: ProjectionRecipe, resume: ResumeTarget)
```

The success path is explicit. There is no implicit fallthrough in the semantic plan.

### 5.12 Projection recipe

```text
ProjectionRecipe =
  SyncedFrame(resume: ResumePoint)
| ReconstructFrame(resume: ResumePoint, entries: ProjectionEntrySet)
| VirtualProjection(resume: ResumePoint, entries: ProjectionEntrySet, dependencies: DependencySet)
```

Current projection support is effectively `SyncedFrame`.

`SyncedFrame` means: the frame is already interpreter-visible. It proves safety, not optimization power.

### 5.13 Projection entry

```text
ProjectionEntry =
  FromSlot(dst: FrameSlotId, src: FrameSlotId, value_type: ValueType)
| FromRegister(dst: FrameSlotId, src: RegisterId, value_type: ValueType)
| Constant(dst: FrameSlotId, value: ConstantValue)
| BoxI64(dst: FrameSlotId, src: Location)
| CopyTValue(dst: FrameSlotId, src: Location)
| PureI64Op(dst: FrameSlotId, op: I64Op, lhs: Location, rhs: Location)
```

Only explicit side-effect-free expression DAGs are allowed inside projection recipes.

### 5.14 Frame state

```text
FrameState =
  SyncedFrameState
| UnsyncedFrameState(pending_writes: PendingWriteSet,
                     virtual_values: VirtualValueSet,
                     live_locations: LiveLocationSet)
```

Current planner work starts mostly in `SyncedFrameState`.

Seam removal moves the plan into `UnsyncedFrameState`. That is only legal if every non-success exit has a reconstructing projection.

### 5.15 Fact state

```text
FactState = {
  signature: Signature64,
  payloads: PayloadLeaseSet,
  dependencies: DependencySet,
}
```

`Signature64` is not enough for shape IDs, field offsets, call targets, or dependency epochs. Those are payload/dependency values, not bits hiding inside the signature.

### 5.16 Fact transfer

```text
FactTransfer = {
  selector: FactState,
  required: FactState,
  checked: FactState,
  produced: FactState,
  killed: FactState,
}
```

For the current `SponFactSig` subset, the signature update is:

```text
facts_out.signature =
  (facts_in.signature & ~killed.signature)
  | produced.signature
  | checked.signature
```

Payload/dependency transfer must be explicit before payload stencils can be accepted.

### 5.17 Dependency obligation

```text
DependencyObligation =
  ShapeEpoch(shape: ShapeId, epoch: EpochId)
| MetatableAbsent(shape: ShapeId, epoch: EpochId)
| FieldOffset(shape: ShapeId, key: FieldKey, offset: FieldOffset)
| ArrayBounds(shape: ShapeId, range: BoundsInfo)
| CallTarget(target: FunctionId, epoch: EpochId)
| BarrierState(cell: BarrierCellId)
```

First planner surface rejects these unless a validation policy explicitly allows them.

### 5.18 Seam candidate and legality

```text
SeamCandidate =
  StoreLoadSeam(producer: StencilInstance,
                consumer: StencilInstance,
                slot: FrameSlotId,
                value: VirtualValueId)
| BoxUnboxSeam(producer: StencilInstance,
               consumer: StencilInstance,
               slot: FrameSlotId,
               value: VirtualValueId)
| RedundantGuardSeam(producer: StencilInstance,
                     consumer: StencilInstance,
                     fact: FactAtom)
```

```text
SeamLegalityResult =
  Removable(proof: SeamRemovalProof)
| BlockedByProjection(exit: EndpointRef)
| BlockedByEffect(effect: EffectConflict)
| BlockedByDependency(dep: DependencyObligation)
| NotACandidate
```

### 5.19 Planner rejection

```text
PlanRejection =
  UnsupportedOp(op: StencilOpKind)
| UnsupportedDataRole(role: DataRole)
| MissingRequiredFact(fact: FactAtom)
| PayloadUnavailable(obligation: PayloadObligation)
| DependencyUnsupported(dep: DependencyObligation)
| MissingProjection(endpoint: EndpointRef)
| ProjectionIncomplete(endpoint: EndpointRef, missing: ProjectionNeed)
| ProjectionSourceClobbered(endpoint: EndpointRef, location: Location)
| EndpointIncompatible(producer: EndpointRef, consumer: EndpointRef)
| AbstractStencilUsedAsExecutable(stencil: StencilId)
| PhysicalAbiMismatch(expected: PhysicalAbiId, actual: PhysicalAbiId)
| VmGateClosed(gate: VmGate)
| NoCandidate(pc: SourcePc)
```

A real implementation may encode this as Lua tagged tables, but the spec names the variants.

### 5.20 Planner deferral

```text
PlannerDeferral =
  NeedsNativeStencils
| NeedsPayloadLeaseModel
| NeedsDependencyInvalidation
| NeedsLalinVmGate(gate: VmGate)
| NeedsProjectionKind(kind: ProjectionKind)
| NeedsEndpointContract(kind: EndpointContractKind)
```


[689 more lines in file. Use offset=545 to continue.]

region check_edge(producer: Endpoint, consumer: Endpoint, live: LiveState;
  compatible: cont(edge: SuccessEdge),
  needs_bridge: cont(requirement: BridgeRequirement),
  incompatible: cont(reason: EndpointIncompatibility))
```

Current descriptors mostly support semantic compatibility, not full physical value-flow compatibility. The outcome must distinguish `needs_bridge` from `incompatible`.

### 6.7 Exit projection validation

```text
region validate_exit_projection(endpoint: Endpoint, frame: FrameState;
  synced_safe: cont(recipe: ProjectionRecipe),
  reconstructable: cont(recipe: ProjectionRecipe),
  incomplete: cont(reason: ProjectionFailure))
```

`SyncedFrame` exits go through `synced_safe`, not `reconstructable`.

### 6.8 Seam analysis

```text
region analyze_seam(seam: SeamCandidate, exits: ExitSet, frame: FrameState;
  removable: cont(proof: SeamRemovalProof),
  blocked_by_projection: cont(exit: EndpointRef),
  blocked_by_effect: cont(effect: EffectConflict),
  blocked_by_dependency: cont(dep: DependencyObligation),
  not_candidate: cont())
```

This protocol is the core optimization gate. It prevents projection presence from being mistaken for projection power.

### 6.9 Report construction

```text
region build_report(trace: PlannerTrace;
  accepted_report: cont(report: PlannerReport),
  rejection_report: cont(report: RejectionReport),
  deferral_report: cont(reason: PlannerDeferral, report: PlannerReport))
```

luajit - <<'LUA'
package.path='./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?.lua;'..package.path
local SSA=require('src.ssa')
local Facts=require('src.facts')
local ops_list={
 { {op='FORPREP',a=0,bx=2,sbx=2} },
 { {op='FORLOOP',a=0,bx=2,sbx=-2} },
 { {op='TFORPREP',a=0,bx=2,sbx=2} },
 { {op='TFORCALL',a=0,b=0,c=1} },
 { {op='TFORLOOP',a=0,bx=2,sbx=-2} },
}
local facts={Facts.fact('type',Facts.slot('R0'),'is_i64',true),Facts.fact('type',Facts.slot('R1'),'is_i64',true),Facts.fact('type',Facts.slot('R2'),'is_i64',true)}
for _,ops in ipairs(ops_list) do
 local r=SSA.compile(ops,facts,{})
 print('---',ops[1].op,'ok',r.ok,'form',table.concat(r.stencil_form,','),'ops',table.concat(r.stencil_ops,','),'sem',table.concat(r.semantic_ops,','))
 for _,n in ipairs(r.stencil.ops or {}) do print(n.id,n.op,n.effect,n.source,n.exit and n.exit.reason or '') end
 for _,h in ipairs(r.stencil_holes or {}) do print('hole',h.id,h.role_kind,h.role_arg,h.op_idx,h.ty) end
 for _,sm in ipairs(r.slotmaps or {}) do print('slotmap',sm.op_idx,sm.logical_slot,sm.field_kind) end
 print('projection',r.projection.exit_obligations,table.concat(r.projection.reasons or {},','))
end
LUA

 for _,h in ipairs(r.stencil_holes or {}) do print('hole',h.id,h.role_kind,h.role_arg,h.op_idx,h.ty) end
 for _,sm in ipairs(r.slotmaps or {}) do print('slotmap',sm.op_idx,sm.logical_slot,sm.field_kind) end
 print('projection',r.projection.exit_obligations,table.concat(r.projection.reasons or {},','))
end

It seems like StencilLower might create a residual endpoint, and that could be okay? I think it can lower residual descriptors for the "FOR" as well. I’ll need to run this through to see how it all works together. There’s a lot to consider here!

eval "$(luarocks --lua-version=5.1 path 2>/dev/null || luarocks path)"; luajit - <<'LUA'
package.path='./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?.lua;'..package.path
local SSA=require('src.ssa')
local StencilLower=require('src.stencil_lower')
for _,op in ipairs({'FORPREP','FORLOOP','TFORPREP','TFORCALL','TFORLOOP'}) do
 local r=SSA.compile({{op=op,a=0,bx=2,sbx=2,b=0,c=1}}, {}, {})
 local lr=StencilLower.generate(r,{})
 print(op,'ssa_ok',r.ok,'lower_ok',lr.ok, lr.errors and table.concat(lr.errors, '|') or '')
 if lr.ok then
   local f=lr.stencil
   print(' endpoints',#f.endpoints,'control',#f.control_relocs,'projections',#f.projections,'data',#f.data_relocs,'len',f.len,'ops',table.concat(f.stencil_ops,','))
   for i,ep in ipairs(f.endpoints) do print('  ep',i,ep.kind,ep.source,ep.exit and ep.exit.reason or '',ep.projection_start,ep.n_projections) end
   for _,cr in ipairs(f.control_relocs) do print('  cr',cr.edge_kind,cr.source,cr.endpoint_index) end
 end
end

local function c_return_variable(i, exit_pc)

    s[#s+1] = string.format("    frame->pc = %s + 1;\n", exit_pc)

local function c_forprep_i64(i, exit_pc)
    local s = {}
    s[#s+1] = string.format("    Value* init_%d = &L->stack[base + a_%d]; Value* step_%d = &L->stack[base + a_%d + 2];\n", i, i, i, i)
    s[#s+1] = string.format("    if (init_%d->tag != %du || step_%d->tag != %du) return %s;\n", i, TAG_INTEGER, i, TAG_INTEGER, exit_pc)
    s[#s+1] = string.format("    init_%d->bits = (uint64_t)((int64_t)init_%d->bits - (int64_t)step_%d->bits);\n", i, i, i)
    s[#s+1] = string.format("    return (size_t)((int64_t)%s + (int64_t)sbx_%d);\n", exit_pc, i)
    return table.concat(s)
end

local function c_forloop_i64(i, exit_pc)
    local s = {}
    s[#s+1] = string.format("    Value* idxv_%d = &L->stack[base + a_%d]; Value* limv_%d = &L->stack[base + a_%d + 1]; Value* stepv_%d = &L->stack[base + a_%d + 2];\n", i, i, i, i, i, i)
    s[#s+1] = string.format("    if (idxv_%d->tag != %du || limv_%d->tag != %du || stepv_%d->tag != %du) return %s;\n", i, TAG_INTEGER, i, TAG_INTEGER, i, TAG_INTEGER, exit_pc)
    s[#s+1] = string.format("    int64_t nidx_%d = (int64_t)idxv_%d->bits + (int64_t)stepv_%d->bits;\n", i, i, i)
    s[#s+1] = string.format("    idxv_%d->bits = (uint64_t)nidx_%d;\n", i, i)
    s[#s+1] = string.format("    int64_t lim_%d = (int64_t)limv_%d->bits; int64_t step_%d = (int64_t)stepv_%d->bits;\n", i, i, i, i)
    s[#s+1] = string.format("    if ((step_%d >= 0 && nidx_%d <= lim_%d) || (step_%d < 0 && nidx_%d >= lim_%d)) { L->stack[base + a_%d + 3] = *idxv_%d; return (size_t)((int64_t)%s + (int64_t)sbx_%d); }\n", i, i, i, i, i, i, i, i, exit_pc, i)
    s[#s+1] = string.format("    return %s + 1;\n", exit_pc)
    return table.concat(s)
end
